ksa_text: Knowledge of data and AI governance structures at the board and executive committee level, including oversight responsibilities, reporting cadences, decision rights allocation, and escalation frameworks.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.

Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although Leadership & Strategy is an edge domain for the VP Analytics, this knowledge KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.473) and conceptual alignment to include at the vp level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.473). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the VP Analytics versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for VP Analytics.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain Leadership & Strategy knowledge KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.473. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the VP Analytics.
